old_claim.note: Electrical Grid (Early Distribution) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: The scoped technology is integration into utility transmission and distribution systems, so an electrical grid is constitutive rather than merely historical background.

source_shape.source_locator: FERC's PURPA overview and qualifying-facility rules concern sales to electric utilities and utility purchase, sale, and interconnection obligations.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The regulatory milestone exists specifically to connect and transact renewable generation through utility electric systems.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Once the node is scoped to power-grid integration rather than renewable generation generally, a grid is part of the definition and must be represented as required.

ontology_before: The electrical grid was represented only as historical background even though the node's defining action is integration into that grid.
ontology_after: The electrical grid is a required direct prerequisite of renewable power grid integration.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-renewable-grid-integration-scope: electrical_grid_early_distribution remains direct and is typed required.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to standalone renewable systems that do not connect to an electrical grid.
short_reason: A grid is constitutive of grid integration, not merely an earlier historical influence.
